title: The Last Great Catch of Fish
summary: “After these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself. There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.” (John 21:1-2).After the resurrection, the Lord came to the Galilee to meet his apostles by the shores of the Galilee. They were six in total. Six represents the day before the Sabbath, a time when the people of God enter into their rest. I believe we are in the sixth day in this time. John does not name two of the apostles. We don’t know why, but this shows we ought not to be disappointed when our names are not mentioned –Jesus knew who they were and they were part of this miracle. “But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No (John 21:4, 5). We must be very careful; in these last days we may be fishing for souls and fruit for the Lord without his help –in our own strength. Since they did not solicit the help of the Lord, they could not even recognize him.
